u_{t,	ext{shear}} \] Additional definitions: \[ R_i =  rac{N^2}{S^2}, \quad S = \sqrt{2 S_{ij} S_{ij}}, \quad N^2 = - rac{g}{ho}  rac{dho}{dz} \] Where: \(
u_{t,R}\): Viscosity due to internal waves (default = \(1 	imes 10^{-4} \, 	ext{m}^2/	ext{s}\)) \(
u_{h,R}\): Diffusivity of heat due to internal waves (default = \(5 	imes 10^{-5} \, 	ext{m}^2/	ext{s}\)) \(
u_{t,	ext{shear}}\): Viscosity due to shear instability (default = \(5 	imes 10^{-3} \, 	ext{m}^2/	ext{s}\)) \(R_{i,	ext{cr}}\): Critical Richardson number (default = 0.7) \(S\): Magnitude of the mean strain rate tensor (units: s\(^{-1}\)) \(N^2\): Brunt–Väisälä frequency squared (units: s\(^{-2}\)) \(g\): Gravitational acceleration (m/s\(^2\)) \(z\): Vertical coordinate (m) Turbulence extinction occurs when the turbulent kinetic energy drops below a threshold \(k < k_{	ext{lim,iw}}\), with the default \(k_{	ext{lim,iw}} = 1 	imes 10^{-6} \, 	ext{m}^2/	ext{s}^2\). The internal wave mixing model is activated using the commands: ! Activate the internal wave mixing model (Kantha and Clayson, 1994) Internal Wave Mixing Model == ON ! {OFF} Optional override of model parameters == klim,iw, Ri,cr, νt,shear, νt,R, νh,R Internal Wave Mixing Model Parameters == 1.0e-6, 0.7, 5.0e-3, 1.0e-4, 5.0e-5 `

F.4 Tracer F.4.1 Science The following sub-sections describe the assignment of a tracer model options: Constant, decay and settling. F.4.1.1 Conservative A tracer is simply advected and dispersed. F.4.1.2 Decay This model allows for the linear temporal decay of a tracer. \[\begin{equation} \tag{F.1} \end{equation}\] DO jtra = 1,Ntrace j = jtra1 + jtra - 1 C = MAX(0.,Ukz(j,2,i3)) / h S(j,i3) = S(j,i3) - Kd(jtra) * C * dZ(i3) END DO F.4.1.3 Settling This model allows for the settling of a tracer. \[\begin{equation} \tag{F.2} \end{equation}\] DO jtra = 1,Ntrace j = jtra1 + jtra - 1 C = MAX(0.,Ukz(j,2,i3)) / h Sbed = ws(jtra) * C Sbed = MIN(Sbed, theta_adv_flux*C*dZ(i3)/dt_) S(j,i3) = S(j,i3) - Sbed END DO

F.5 Horizontal Mixing F.5.1 Science The following sub-sections describe the scalar mixing model options: none, constant, Elder, Smagorinsky and warmup. These models set or compute the scalar diffusivity \(K^{AD}_d\) (also referred to as eddy diffusivity in the literature) that is multiplicative to the diffusive terms in the scalar conservation equations. \[\begin{equation} \nabla \cdot \left( K^{AD}_d \nabla \phi \right) \tag{F.3} \end{equation}\] Where: - \(K^{AD}_d\) = scalar diffusivity \([\text{m}^2\!/\text{s}]\) - \(\phi\) = scalar concentration \([\text{mass}\!/\text{m^3}]\) F.5.1.1 None This model sets the value of \(K^{AD}_d\) to be zero. F.5.1.2 Constant This model sets the value of \(K^{AD}_d\) to be constant. F.5.1.3 Elder \[\begin{equation} \tag{F.4} \end{equation}\] \[\begin{equation} \tag{F.5} \end{equation}\] \[\begin{equation} \tag{F.6} \end{equation}\] ! CALCULATE DEPTH AVERAGED VELOCITY IF (hL>hWet.AND.hR>hWet) THEN DO n = 0,NL(i2)-1 u = u + 0.5*(UL(2,i3+n)/hL*dZ(1,i3+n)+UR(2,i3+n)/hR*dZ(2,i3+n)) v = v + 0.5*(UL(3,i3+n)/hL*dZ(1,i3+n)+UR(3,i3+n)/hR*dZ(2,i3+n)) END DO u = u / h v = v / h END IF umag = SQRT(u**2+v**2)+small Dl = MAX(Ds_coef(1,i2),small) Dt = MAX(Ds_coef(2,i2),small) IF (bot_drag_mod==1) THEN ! MANNING's ROUGHNESS f = MAX(h**0.83333333 * SQRT(g) * bot_drag_coeff(i2) / umag, 0.) ELSEIF (bot_drag_mod==2) THEN ! ROUGHNESS LENGTH / LOG-LAW f = MAX(kappa * h / MAX(log(11. * h / bot_drag_coeff(i2)) * umag, small), 0.) END IF Dtmp = MAX(umag*umag*f,small) Dl = MAX(Dl, Ds_limits(1,i2) / Dtmp) Dl = MIN(Dl, Ds_limits(2,i2) / Dtmp) Dt = MAX(Dt, Ds_limits(1,i2) / Dtmp) Dt = MIN(Dt, Ds_limits(2,i2) / Dtmp) Ds(1,i3) = (Dl*u**2 + Dt*v**2)*f Ds(2,i3) = (Dl-Dt)*u*v*f Ds(3,i3) = (Dl*v**2 + Dt*u**2)*f DO n = 1,NL(i2)-1 Ds(:,i3+n) = Ds(:,i3) END DO F.5.1.4 Smagorinsky \[\begin{equation} \tag{F.7} \end{equation}\] DO i2 = 1,SIZE(idx3) i3 = idx3(i2) DO n = 1,NL(i2) dudx = gradu(1,2,i3) dudy = gradu(2,2,i3) dvdx = gradu(1,3,i3) dvdy = gradu(2,3,i3) D = (Ds_coef(1,i2) * Lsmag(i3))**2 * SQRT(dudx**2+dvdy**2+0.5*(dudy+dvdx)**2) ! IF (ISNAN(D)) D = 0._SGL D = MIN(MAX(D,Ds_limits(1,i2)),Ds_limits(2,i2)) Ds(1,i3) = D Ds(3,i3) = D i3 = i3 + 1 END DO END DO !$OMP END DO NOWAIT F.5.1.5 Warmup \[\begin{equation} \tag{F.8} \end{equation}\] DO i3 = 1,SIZE(Ds,2) Ds(1,i3) = MIN(CFL,0.25) * CFL_dx(i3)**2 / dt Ds(2,i3) = 0. Ds(3,i3) = Ds(1,i3) !Ds(:,i3) = MIN(Ds(:,i3),1000.) !Ds(:,i3) = MAX(Ds(:,i3),0.) END DO

F.6 Computational Timestep F.6.1 Context F.6.2 Science F.6.2.1 Model Timestep Timestep calculations for advection dispersion (in either 2D and 3D) include an addition scalar diffusivity criterion \(\Delta t_s\). \[\begin{equation} \Delta t_s \;\leq\; \frac{(L^*)^2}{|D_{xx} n_x| + |D_{yy} n_y|} \tag{F.9} \end{equation}\] Where: - \(D_{xx}\) = diffusivity in the \(x\)-direction \([\text{m}^2\!/\text{s}]\) - \(D_{yy}\) = diffusivity in the \(y\)-direction \([\text{m}^2\!/\text{s}]\) - \(n_x, n_y\) = components of the face unit normal AD With 2D Hydrodynamics The maximum allowable internal and external timesteps for advection dispersion in 2D are presented in Equations (F.10) and (F.11) respectively. These are consistent with the 2D HD only equations presented in Appendix D.7.1.2 with the addition scalar diffusivity criterion \(\Delta t_s\). \[\begin{equation} \Delta t_{\max\_internal} \;=\; \min\!\left( \frac{1}{\tfrac{1}{\Delta t_u} + \tfrac{1}{\Delta t_d}},\; \frac{1}{\tfrac{1}{\Delta t_u} + \tfrac{1}{\Delta t_s}} \right) \tag{F.10} \end{equation}\] \[\begin{equation} \Delta t_{\max\_external} \;=\; \min\!\left( \frac{1}{\tfrac{1}{\Delta t_c} + \tfrac{1}{\Delta t_d}},\; \frac{1}{\tfrac{1}{\Delta t_u} + \tfrac{1}{\Delta t_s}} \right) \tag{F.11} \end{equation}\] The resulting \(\Delta t_{\max\_internal}\) and \(\Delta t_{\max\_external}\) are applied in Equations (D.9) and (D.10) when using the global CFL command. If the split commands CFL Internal and CFL External are used, then they are applied in Equations (D.11) and (D.12), as described in Appendix D.7.1.2. AD With 2D Hydrodynamics The maximum allowable internal and external timesteps for advection dispersion in 3D are presented in Equations (F.15) and (F.12) respectively. These are consistent with the 3D HD only Equations (D.7) and (D.8) with the addition of the \(\Delta t_s\) term. \[\begin{equation} \Delta t_{\max\_external} \;=\; \min\!\left( \frac{1}{\tfrac{1}{\Delta t_c} + \tfrac{1}{\Delta t_d(2D)}},\; \frac{1}{\tfrac{1}{\Delta t_u(2D)} + \tfrac{1}{\Delta t_s(2D)}} \right) \tag{F.12} \end{equation}\] \[\begin{equation} \Delta t_{\max\_internal-h} \;=\; \min\left( \frac{1}{\tfrac{1}{\Delta t_u(i)} + \tfrac{1}{\Delta t_d(i)}},\;\frac{1}{\tfrac{1}{\Delta t_u(i)} + \tfrac{1}{\Delta t_s(i)}} \right) \tag{F.13} \end{equation}\] \[\begin{equation} \Delta t_{\max\_internal-v} \;=\; \min\left( \frac{\min\!\big(\Delta z^{+}_k,\;\Delta z^{-}_k\big)}{|w_k|} \right) \tag{F.14} \end{equation}\] \[\begin{equation} \Delta t_{\max\_internal} \;=\; \min\!\left( \Delta t_{\max\_internal-h},\; \Delta t_{\max\_internal-v} \right) \tag{F.15} \end{equation}\] Where: \(\Delta t_{\max\_internal-h}\) maximum allowable horizontal internal timestep (s) \(\Delta t_{\max\_internal-v}\) maximum allowable vertical internal timestep (s) \(\Delta t_u(i),\, \Delta t_d(i),\, \Delta t_s(i)\) are maximum allowable timestep limits at each 3D horizontal cell face \(i\) (s) \(\Delta t_u(k)\) is the maximum allowable advective timestep limit at each 3D vertical cell face \(k\) (s) \(\Delta z^{+}_k\) = thickness of the cell above face \(k\) (m) \(\Delta z^{-}_k\) = thickness of the cell below face \(k\) (m) \(w_k\) = vertical velocity at face \(k\) (m/s) The resulting \(\Delta t_{\max\_internal}\) and \(\Delta t_{\max\_external}\) are applied in Equations (D.9) and (D.10) when using the global CFL command. If the split commands CFL Internal and CFL External are used, then they are applied in Equations (D.11) and (D.12), as described in Appendix D.7.1.2.

F.7 Atmospheric Heat Exchange F.7.1 Context F.7.2 Shortwave Radiation Model F.7.2.1 Model 1 Science manual eqn 32 \tag{F.16} \tag{F.17} F.7.2.2 Model 2 Science manual section 5.1.2 F.7.3 Longwave Radiation Model F.7.3.1 Model 1 F.7.3.2 Model 2 Science Manual S 5.2.2 F.7.3.3 Model 3 Science Manual S 5.2.3 F.7.3.4 Model 4 Science Manual S 5.2.4 F.7.3.5 Model 5 Science Manual S 5.2.5 F.7.4 Latent Heat Model F.7.4.1 Vapour Pressure and Specific Humidity F.7.4.1.1 Magnus-Tetens Science Manual S 5.3.1.1 F.7.4.1.2 Lowe and Reed Science Manual S 5.3.1.2 F.7.4.2 Bulk Aerodynamic Latent Heat Transfer Coefficient F.7.4.2.1 Constant Constant F.7.4.2.2 Kondo Kondo IF (w10 < 2.2) THEN ch = 1.185*(w10**(-0.157)) ce = 1.230*(w10**(-0.160)) ELSEIF (w10 >= 2.2 .AND. w10 < 5.0) THEN ch = 0.927 + 0.0546 * w10 ce = 0.969 + 0.0521 * w10 ELSEIF (w10 >= 5.0 .AND. w10 < 8.0) THEN ch = 1.150 + 0.0100 * w10 ce = 1.180 + 0.0100 * w10 ELSEIF (w10 >= 8.0 .AND. w10 < 25.0) THEN ch = 1.170 + 0.0075 * w10 - 0.00045*((w10-8.0)**2) ce = 1.196 + 0.0080 * w10 + 0.00040*((w10-8.0)**2) ELSEIF (w10 >= 25.0) THEN ch = 1.652 - 0.017 * w10 ce = 1.680 - 0.016 * w10 ENDIF ch = 1e-3*ch ce = 1e-3*ce IF (.NOT.PRESENT(wtemp) .OR. .NOT.PRESENT(atemp) .OR. .NOT.PRESENT(es) .OR. .NOT.PRESENT(eair)) RETURN SELECT CASE (atm_stab_model) CASE(1) ! Calculate stability parameter stab = ((wtemp - atemp) + 0.61*(atemp + 0.0098*zrefa)*(es - eair)) / & (w10 ** 2) / ((1+log10(10./zrefa))**2) stab = stab * abs(stab)/(abs(stab) + 0.01) ! Correcting cdref for the stability IF (stab < -3.3) THEN ! very stable stab_coefw = 0.0 stab_coef = stab_coefw ELSEIF(stab >= -3.3 .AND. stab < 0) THEN ! slightly stable stab_coefw = 0.1 + 0.03*stab + 0.9*EXP(4.8*stab) stab_coef = stab_coefw ELSEIF (stab >= 0.) THEN ! unstable stab_coefw = 1.0 + 0.47*SQRT(stab) stab_coef = 1.0 + 0.63*SQRT(stab) ENDIF cd10 = cd10 * stab_coefw ch = ch * stab_coef ce = ce * stab_coef END SELECT F.7.4.3 Latent Heat of Vaporisation Science Manual S 5.3.2.3 F.7.5 Sensible Heat Model F.7.5.1 Bulk Aerodynamic Sensible Heat Transfer Coefficient F.7.5.1.1 Constant Science Manual S 5.4 F.7.5.1.2 Kondo
